1.notes: Opening par 4 at Seminole Legacy. Nicklaus design with 50ft elevation changes throughout course.

2.notes: Medium par 4 with mature pines lining both sides.

3.notes: Long par 3 over water. One of the signature holes at Seminole Legacy.

4.notes: Reachable par 5 for long hitters. Dogleg left with bunkers guarding the corner.

5.notes: Short par 3 with challenging green complex.

6.notes: Long par 5 with dramatic elevation change. Risk-reward second shot over water.

7.notes: Medium par 4 with tight landing area.

9.notes: Demanding par 4 finishing the front nine. Hardest handicap hole on course.

10.notes: Strong par 4 to start the back nine.

11.notes: Reachable par 5 with water on the right side.

12.notes: Long par 4 requiring two solid shots.

13.notes: Long par 3 with bunkers surrounding the green.

14.notes: Longest hole on the course. Three-shot par 5 for most players.

15.notes: Short par 3. Easiest hole on the course but don't be fooled by the green.

16.notes: Medium par 4 with water in play for aggressive players.

17.notes: Long par 4 with a well-bunkered green complex.

18.notes: Finishing hole with dramatic uphill approach to green fronting the clubhouse. Second hardest hole.

1.notes: Opening par 5 at Golf Club of Georgia Lakeside. Arthur Hills design in Atlanta area.

3.notes: Short par 3 over water to peninsula green.

4.notes: Medium par 4 with bunkers guarding the green.

6.notes: Long par 3 with bunkers short and right.

7.notes: Strong par 4 requiring accuracy off the tee. Hardest handicap hole.

9.notes: Reachable par 5 finishing the front nine. Risk-reward with water.

10.notes: Long par 4 to start the back nine.

11.notes: Longest hole on course. Three-shot par 5 with dramatic water feature.

12.notes: Medium par 4 with tight landing area.

14.notes: Long par 4 with water on approach.

15.notes: Medium par 4 with bunkers on both sides.

16.notes: Hardest hole on course. Dogleg left with water on second shot.

17.notes: Long par 3 with dramatic elevation drop.

18.notes: Finishing par 5 with risk-reward second shot. Water guards the green.

1.notes: Opening par 4 at University of Louisville. Spencer Holt design in Kentucky.

3.notes: Long par 3 requiring a solid iron.

4.notes: Longest hole on the course. Dramatic par 5 with water hazard.

6.notes: Short par 4 that plays even shorter with downhill.

7.notes: Long par 4 with dogleg right around bunkers.

9.notes: Reachable par 5 finishing the front nine. Risk-reward over water.

10.notes: Medium par 4 to start the back nine.

11.notes: Long par 4 requiring two solid shots.

12.notes: Long par 3 with water short of green.

13.notes: Reachable par 5 with water in play for second shot.

15.notes: Medium par 4 with bunkers guarding the green.

17.notes: Par 5 with risk-reward second shot over water.

18.notes: Finishing par 4 with dramatic uphill approach to green.
